rows.text: when i see posts like from people like “ i just went to china for the first time and here are ten things i learnt “ it’s all just that you have to use qr codes to pay and buying train tickets is a faff and some places are poor and some are rich . does nobody do anything fun

got the train to hangzhou, realised half way along the line that i had a flight today and not tomorrow, had to add an extra night to my hotel as i wouldn’t make it back in time for checkout, train station i arrived at didn’t have tickets back so im currently on the tube 1h over

spent several hours going to two hospitals and three pharmacies to try to get a prescription medication and was told that none of them carry it . found out that on jd i can just pay 9.9 yuan for a doctor to prescribe and issue the medication in < 2 minutes

there was some form of ai doctor triaging me, which asked for my medical history etc and my symptoms, which then put me “on hold” for a minute before a real doctor wrote me the script . wonderful . the medication was 70 yuan for a 1.5 weeks’ supply

I was prepping an event and struggling. Got unblocked writing it when I found my voice: writing it in prophetic perfect tense.
I titled it "A story to be written, a story that will never be lived" and wrote in the past tense what happened at the event. And then we ran the event.

Prism: Spectral-Aware Block-Sparse Attention

A training-free method that accelerates long-context LLM pre-filling up to 5.1× on 128K contexts with negligible accuracy loss. Solves RoPE's "blind spot" by disentangling attention into high and low frequency bands.
